diagrams.title: UI/UX intake authority boundary
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Author[Story author] --> Intake[UI/UX intake artifact]
  Intake --> Coverage[Coverage summary]
  Brief[Free-form brief] --> Coverage
  Coverage --> Plan[design-modernize plan]
  Plan --> Review[PR and review artifacts]
  Threat[Unverified vague or conflicting UI direction] --> Intake
  Intake --> Boundary[Authority boundary]
  Boundary --> Code[Current route code and verified contracts]
  Boundary --> Spec[VibePro story, spec, and architecture artifacts]

clauses.statement: When a story needs UI/UX intake, VibePro writes story-scoped JSON, Markdown, and coverage artifacts under .vibepro/uiux/<story-id>/.
clauses.rationale: The CLI command must create a durable intake starting point before design-modernize planning.

test_refs.anchor: uiux intake template and validate writes story-scoped coverage

test_pattern.must_cover: uiux intake template and validate writes story-scoped coverage

clauses.statement: The intake schema includes required fields for product/service, users, jobs, purpose, scope, authority, style, tone, color, typography, component, state, spacing, responsive, accessibility, and token policy.
clauses.rationale: UI/UX work needs structured inputs rather than vague aesthetic requests.

clauses.statement: When intake validation runs, VibePro reports field coverage using explicit, inferred, missing, and not_applicable statuses in machine-readable JSON.
clauses.rationale: Coverage must be inspectable by later gates and not hidden in prose.

test_refs.anchor: uiux intake template and validate writes story-scoped coverage

clauses.statement: design-modernize plan resolves the story intake when present and writes .vibepro/design-modernize/<story-id>/uiux-intake-coverage.json.
clauses.rationale: Structured UI/UX inputs must be part of design-modernize evidence rather than a separate manual note.

test_refs.anchor: design-modernize plan writes UI/UX intake coverage and flags vague-only briefs

clauses.statement: When a plan only receives a vague free-form brief, VibePro keeps the plan command usable but marks coverage as needs_intake_detail with intake guidance.
clauses.rationale: Free-form briefs remain supported, but vague aesthetic-only direction cannot be treated as complete UI/UX input.

clauses.statement: Coverage output states that current route code, verified contracts, screenshots, and VibePro story/spec/architecture artifacts win over conflicting intake text.
clauses.rationale: Structured intake is reference material and must not become an unchecked implementation authority.
